rag_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report unresolved references or type errors, trace the root cause to invalid identifiers or mismatched types in the same line and fix or remove the offending clause.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex when writing AGREE contracts to avoid 'Couldn't resolve reference' errors.
Ensure AGREE scalar types match AADL expectations: int, real, and bool for constants and equations.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always validate that referenced identifiers are visible in the current scope and that types align between declarations and expressions.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes unless explicitly supported by the model's configured dependencies.
When fixing AGREE diagnostics, prefer minimal repairs that correct the root cause rather than adding new architecture elements.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in appropriate component type or implementation scopes.
Assignments in implementation scope should only reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve type mismatches by introducing new architecture elements; instead, correct the type or remove the invalid clause.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs. implementations
Assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes
